raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assemblywoman Sharon Quirk-Silva has secured $1.7 million in funding for a STEM education program to expand the STARBASE youth program at the Joint Forces Training Base in Los Alamitos.",
    "STARBASE Los Alamitos is anticipated to host 3,000 students from 105 fifth grade classes in the 2017-18 school year, with a waiting list of 46 classes.",
    "The funding will allow for the construction of a new 3,500 square foot building at the National Guard Base at Los Alamitos which will include two STARBASE classrooms.",
    "The new classrooms will allow an additional 2,000 students to attend the STARBASE program each year, eliminating the waiting list for potential students.",
    "STARBASE teaches students to complete simulated missions using skills and principles from Physics, Computer Science, Chemistry, and Statistics.",
    "Students in the STARBASE program program a robot to complete a Mars Rover mission.",
    "Students design prototypes using Computer Aided Design (CAD) in the STARBASE program.",
    "STARBASE runs a competitive rocket team that participates in Team America Rocket Challenge while learning the basics of rocket science.",
    "The STARBASE program is now in 59 locations across 30 states, exposing local fifth-grade elementary students to real-world applications of STEM skills.",
    "The STARBASE Youth Program Facility at the Joint Forces Training Base in Los Alamitos serves nearly 4,000 students a year.",
    "The STARBASE program has served more than 1 million students nationwide since its inception.",
    "The lack of STEM educated youth in America has been identified as a future national security issue by the Department of Defense."
  ]
}
```
